S Skillmaker Studio
Docs GitHub

0:00 · the pull

Your agent did something weird again.

No crash. No error. Nothing in the logs — just off, in front of someone who matters. And you can't say why, which version did it, or what changed.

0:05 · not anymore

Skillmaker Studio puts receipts on every skill: what it passed, on which version, on which model.

When something goes weird, you know first, you can say why, and you fix it on the same surface.

0:10 · watch

Ninety seconds, start to receipt.

30–60s demo goes here
storyboard — not captured footage
  1. 01

    $ skillmaker adopt

    Point it at the repo you already have. Skills become measurable in place — zero files moved.

  2. 02

    the board

    Every skill, its stage, its last honest number. Nothing orphaned, nothing in a tab you can't find.

  3. 03

    designing an eval

    You and the agent decide what “good” means, in plain English. No evals background.

  4. 04

    $ skillmaker run

    Real runs on the real agent. n, pass rate, confidence interval — pinned to the exact version and model.

  5. 05

    a number moves

    New model ships; a skill dips below its floor. You find out from the board — not from a customer.

  6. 06

    the operating table

    Trail back to why. Fix the process, not just the prompt. Re-measure. Receipt attached.

0:30 · the secret

The secret is the trail.

Every number is pinned to the exact skill version and model that earned it. Every design choice is linked to the evidence behind it. So when a skill is stable but wrong — running clean, doing the job badly — you can see it, trace it, and fix the process, not just the prompt.

1:00 · sound familiar?

It's not just the weird Tuesday.

  • A new model just shipped.
  • A teammate “improved” your skill.
  • You inherited a repo of skills you didn't write.
  • Someone ran yours on a model it never met.
  • Your first commercial skill is due.
  • Your team wants one blessed set.

Same pull. Same fix.

1:30 · you're already objecting. good.

“Do we have to restructure our repo?”

No. adopt works in place — 59/60 and 39/39 skills adopted cleanly on real public repos. Zero files moved.

“I'm not an evals person.”

You don't need to be. Our agents co-design the evals with you, in plain English. You judge; they build.

“Won't the numbers flatter you?”

They refuse to. Three runs reads “below smoke” on our own case study — even when the percentage looks great.

“Where does my data go?”

Nowhere. Free, local, MIT-licensed, no account. It phones nothing home.

1:59 · the next ninety seconds

Find out first.

ninety seconds 
$ curl -fsSL https://skillmaker.studio/install.sh | sh
$ skillmaker adopt

Free. Local. MIT. Phones nothing home. On the repo you already have.

Want hands-on help? We're working with a small first round of design partners — open an issue and tell us what broke. Running skills as a team? Talk to us — we're designing the multi-player studio with a handful of companies.

We can't make your agent predictable — nobody can; that's what you hired it for. We can make sure nothing drifts silently, nothing fails anonymously, and nothing stays wrong.

The only thing you risk is finding out.
